full_text:
Mercury surges above normal in Bengaluru as westerlies linger; rain likely from April 11
At 5.30 pm, the maximum temperature in Bengaluru City was 35.2°C, about 2.2°C above normal. The HAL airport and the Kempegowda International Airport (KIA) each recorded 35°C, an increase of 1.3°C and 0.3°C, respectively.
